title: wurmbea punctata
intro: Wurmbea punctata, also known as the Common Spiranth or Spiranthes australis, is a flowering plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It is a type of orchid that belongs to the family Colchicaceae and is commonly found in wetlands and grasslands. The medicinal properties of Wurmbea punctata include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
